content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes two numbers and an operator (+, -, *, /) as input and performs the corresponding operation using a switch statement. Print the result upto 2 decimal places.
constraints: Operator can only be one of the following: +, -, *, /
Division by zero is not allowed
content.inputFormat: First line contains two space-separated numbers. Second line contains an operator (+, -, *, /).
content.outputFormat: Print the result of the operation on a new line, rounded to two decimal places.

solution.approach: Read two numbers and an operator from the input. Use a switch statement to perform the corresponding operation and print the result.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) because the number of operations is constant
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) because the space used does not grow with the input size

code.Python3:
num1, num2 = map(float, input().split())
operator = input().strip()

# Perform the operation using a switch statement
match operator:
    case '+':
        result = num1 + num2
    case '-':
        result = num1 - num2
    case '*':
        result = num1 * num2
    case '/':
        if num2 != 0:
            result = num1 / num2
        else:
            print("Error: Division by zero")
            exit()
    case _:
        print("Invalid operator")
        exit()

print(f"{result:.2f}")
